name: Chicken Souvlaki with Tzatziki Sauce
method: Put the lime juice, olive oil, vinegar, oregano, black pepper in a large
In a different medium dish, put the cubes of chicken.
Combine with rubbed garlic and seven teaspoons of dressing.
Toss the chicken to coat equally, cover, and cool for around 2 hours to
marinate, flipping periodically.
Put aside the rest of the dressing for the salad.
Work on the tzatziki sauce as the chicken marinates: put the cubed
cucumbers in a fine-mesh sieve set over the dish.
Mix with ½ teaspoon salt and let it rest for about thirty minutes to
drain.
Clean the cucumbers gently with a clean cloth, put them in a large
bowl, and mix them with the garlic, dairy, and dill.
Sprinkle with salt to satisfy. To eat, put it in the fridge until ready.
Bits of chicken on 12 to 15 skewers. Discard the marinade
Take barbecue pan and barbecue over moderate flame.
Put chicken skewers on the grill until well golden brown. The
core temperature reads 155°, rotating equally to grill in all directions
on the instantly read thermometer, about five minutes in total.
Remove the chicken from the serving dish and let it sit for
three minutes.
In the meantime, barbecue the pitas momentarily and keep
them warm.
Start preparing a salad well before serving: swirl the olive oil
into the stored chicken marinade.
Add the cabbage, tomato, cucumber, parsley, onions, and feta.
With salt and black pepper, scatter.
Serve the salad, pita, and tzatziki sauce on skewers.
Take the chicken from the skewers and cram it with sauces
and salad into the pitas.
